items.title: XRP ETFs add $35 million as bitcoin and ether funds lost $2 billion in late May
items.summary: From May 20 to May 29, XRP funds took in $35 million while bitcoin and ether ETFs lost roughly $2 billion combined, with Ripple’s earlier reported XRP treasury plan still awaiting confirmation.

items.title: Spot Bitcoin ETFs see record 10-day outflow streak, analyst calls it ‘contrarian indicator’
items.summary: Spot Bitcoin ETFs logged a record 10-day outflow streak totaling nearly $3 billion, while Ether ETFs bled for 14 consecutive sessions.

items.title: Bitcoin treasury space still has fair share of ‘carnival barkers’: BSTR founder
items.summary: BSTR co-founder Sean Bill says many Bitcoin treasury companies lack the “ability to actually deploy Bitcoin.”

items.title: US has seized nearly $1 billion in Iranian crypto, Treasury secretary says
items.summary: US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the US has seized roughly $1 billion in Iranian crypto assets, double the figure disclosed in late April.

items.title: Bitcoin, ether, XRP, dogecoin lag a nine-week stocks rally as ETF demand cools
items.summary: The S&P 500 posted its longest weekly winning streak since 2023 and Brent oil stabilized near $92 on US-Iran ceasefire hopes. The biggest cryptocurrencies still drifted lower, with Hyperliquid's HYPE the only major name to rally.

items.title: Bitcoin’s biggest quantum risk may not be wallet keys. An early investor fears something bigger
items.summary: Andrew Gault, the venture capitalist who funded the quantum hardware labs now threatening bitcoin, says the industry is looking in the wrong place. Google's own security team moved in the same direction in March.

items.title: Bitcoin retail sentiment still matters, says Swan Bitcoin CEO
items.summary: Swan Bitcoin CEO Cory Klippsten said it is important to pay attention to retail sentiment around Bitcoin, as ownership is still far from concentrated and “it’s not like Blackrock owns the Bitcoin.”
